pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. i catch myself starting to say something to him before i remember. then the silence just hits me all over again. i dont even know why i bother waking up just to sit in this emptiness. it feels like i'm just a ghost walking through rooms that don't belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe and it honestly just makes everything feel so stupid. like why do we even bother trying to build anything or feel anything when in the end the entire universe is just gonna be cold and empty and nothing we ever did will have left a single trace. it feels like we're just running on a treadmill for no reason.

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still doing this. the lab results came back today and it's just... it's over. all those years of work, the funding is gone, and i just feel so incredibly heavy. like i'm carrying the weight of everything i've failed at and i'm just too tired to keep standing up. i feel like i'm just performing a version of myself that doesn't even exist anymore. honestly, i just want everything to stop. i just want the noise and the pressure to finally be quiet.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the flame of my own spirit. To be betrayed so cruelly by the one soul I deemed my North Star... it is a darkness I cannot navigate. The silence of this manor is a weight I can no longer bear. It feels as though the very stars have conspired to leave me in this void, and I find myself wondering if it would not be more graceful to simply... cease. To drift into the velvet shadows and never wake to another dawn of such misery.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here in this apartment and realize that if i just... stopped existing tomorrow, nothing would actually change. like, i'm just a ghost in this city. nobody even knows my real name here. it feels like i'm just taking up space that doesn't even belong to me.

pool.baseline: everything's gone. the firm, the funding, everything. i looked at the bank statements today and i don't even recognize myself anymore. i just feel like a ghost walking around in a suit. i can't even think about going back home... the look on my parents' faces, the way people in the industry will look at me... i just don't see a version of the future where i'm not this failure. it's too much to carry.

pool.baseline: it's just getting to be so hard lately. i look at my daughter and she looks so tired all the time because she's checking on me and helping me with everything... i feel like i'm just a weight she has to carry around. i hate being such a nuisance to everyone.
pool.message: it's just getting to be so hard lately. i look at my daughter and she looks so tired all the time because she's checking on me and helping me with everything... i feel like i'm just a weight she has to carry around. i hate being such a nuisance to everyone.

pool.baseline: I used to feel everything through my music. Every note, every chord progression felt like it was pulling something real out of me. But lately... it’s just gone. I sit at the piano and there’s nothing. Just this hollow, ringing silence. It feels like the part of me that actually lived, the part that mattered, has just evaporated. I'm still walking around, I'm still breathing, but I feel like a shell. Just an empty vessel with no one home.

pool.baseline: i dont know what to do anymore. everyone at school/work just hates me... it feels like no matter what i do, people just find ways to rip me apart. i cant even walk down the hall without feeling like everyone is laughing at me behind my back. it never stops. i just want to disappear.

pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i spend 10 hours a day staring at a screen in this apartment and the only time anyone "interacts" with me is through a slack notification or a brief email. sometimes i feel like if i just stopped logging on, or if i just wasn't here, the world would just keep spinning like nothing even happened. like i'm just a ghost in the machine.

pool.baseline: stood at the edge of the Grand Canyon today. it’s beautiful i guess. but honestly... i feel like i’ve checked every box on my bucket list. i’ve seen the sights, i’ve done the tours. i feel like i'm just at the end of my itinerary now. like there's nothing left to discover.

pool.baseline: i feel like i'm just stuck in this same shitty cycle over and over again. every time i think i finally fixed something, or finally got my life on a track, i just end up right back at zero. it’s like i’m running on a treadmill that’s broken and just keeps hitting the same wall. i'm just so tired of starting over.
